ACT — Education policy (current, from act.org.nz/education)

Our Values: Every child should have access to a quality education that equips them with the knowledge and skills to succeed. We believe in empowering parents to choose the best education for their children, whether public, private, or partnership school. We support high standards, real accountability, and choice for parents. Public schools should focus on teaching core knowledge in reading, writing, maths, science, and history, alongside building resilience and character.

Achievements ACT cites: Restoring partnership (charter) schools — publicly funded but independently run. Scrapping "Modern Learning Environments" (ending the open-plan classroom experiment). Refocusing the curriculum on core knowledge — strengthening literacy and numeracy. Pushing for accountability in NCEA reform / lifting assessment standards. Promoting school attendance — improving reporting and enforcement to reduce truancy.

Next goals: Introduce a Student Education Account system — every child to have a publicly funded account parents can use at any registered school or provider, giving families choice. Publish detailed, comparable school performance data so parents can make informed choices. Raise teaching standards — teacher training focused on evidence-based methods for literacy and numeracy. Reward the best teachers — performance-based pay enabling schools to pay their best teachers more based on student progress.
